records.ety: [OE. regne, OF. reigne, regne, F. rgne, fr. L. regnum, fr. rex, regis, a king, fr. regere to guide, rule. See Regal, Regimen.]

defs: Royal authority; supreme power; sovereignty; rule; dominion.
quotes: He who like a father held his reign.
Saturn's sons received the threefold reign Of heaven, of ocean, and deep hell beneath.

defs: The territory or sphere which is reigned over; kingdom; empire; realm; dominion.
quotes: [God] him bereft the regne that he had.

defs: The time during which a king, queen, or emperor possesses the supreme authority; as, it happened in the reign of Elizabeth.

records.ety: [OE. regnen, reinen, OF. regner, F. rgner, fr. L. regnare, fr. regnum. See Reign, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Reigned (r?nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Reigning.]

defs: To possess or exercise sovereign power or authority; to exercise government, as a king or emperor;; to hold supreme power; to rule.
quotes: We will not have this man to reign over us.
Shall Banquo's issue ever Reign in this kingdom?

defs: Hence, to be predominant; to prevail.
To have superior or uncontrolled dominion; to rule.
quotes: Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body.
